content:
Case 17:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 44
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Scheduled follow-up after alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T for B-ALL, with interest in outpatient treatment options
- Vital signs: Temp 36.9 °C, BP not reported at this visit, HR not reported at this visit, RR not reported at this visit, SpO₂ not reported at this visit.
Setting: Outpatient hematology/oncology follow-up after alloSCT and CAR-T
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in a routine follow-up setting after intensive leukemia therapy. You can review prior history and recent clinical course, perform a targeted examination, and request standard follow-up evaluations appropriate to an ambulatory visit. Decisions focus on surveillance and whether further workup for relapse or complications is indicated.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

response.request: Summary of leukemia history and recent treatment course (diagnosis subtype/cytogenetics, dates and outcomes of induction/consolidation, alloSCT details incl donor/conditioning/GVHD, CAR-T product/date/toxicities, current disease status/MRD results)

content:
'treatment_history' available in round 3.
This request asks for a detailed prior-treatment summary (induction/consolidation, alloSCT details, CAR‑T, etc.). That item is not available in this round. Consider requesting current available items such as the history/physical, CBC/chemistry, or baseline imaging; cytogenetics/chimerism and bone marrow reports will be available in later rounds.

content:
Cbc Chemistry:
Hb 10.6 g/dL, WBC 3.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 98 × 10⁹/L; MCV 92 fL, MCH 29 pg.
Differential: neutrophils 42%, lymphocytes 48%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.
Coagulation: INR 1.1, aPTT 30 sec.
Chemistry: Na 138 mmol/L, K 4.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, BUN 19 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 220 U/L, uric acid 5.2 mg/dL, CRP 0.6 mg/dL.
Cardiac biomarkers: NT-proBNP 460 pg/mL, troponin T 0.012 ng/mL.

content:
History Physical:
2024-07-04: The patient presented for scheduled follow-up, reporting stable overall condition. He denied fever, night sweats, or weight loss. No exertional dyspnea, abdominal pain, or neurological complaints were reported. The patient informed the team that he had stopped some medications. He actively inquired about “next treatment options,” emphasizing his preference for outpatient-based regimens.
Current medication: aciclovir 400 mg 1-0-1, posaconazole stopped, 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 960 mg three times per week (taken irregularly), bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-1, amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-1, atorvastatin 10 mg (stopped), folic acid 5 mg 1-0-0, magnesium 300 mg 1-0-0, and various nutritional supplements.

content:
Baseline Imaging:
2024-07-09: Abdominal ultrasound showed normal liver and spleen size, no lymphadenopathy, and no ascites.
Echocardiography demonstrated preserved left ventricular function (LVEF 62%), without pericardial effusion.
Sinus CT revealed bilateral maxillary sinus opacification with suspicion of fungal superinfection.

solve.working_diagnosis: Clinically stable post–alloSCT and post–CD19 CAR-T B-ALL patient with persistent pancytopenia (anemia, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia) and incidental sinus CT findings suspicious for fungal sinusitis; relapse cannot be assessed in round 1 and requires marrow/MRD evaluation in subsequent rounds.
differentials: Post-transplant/CAR-T cytopenias (marrow suppression, poor graft function)
Viral reactivation (e.g., CMV/EBV) or other infection-related marrow suppression
Invasive fungal rhinosinusitis vs chronic sinusitis with fungal colonization

treatment_plan.action: Urgent ENT referral for nasal endoscopy and sampling (culture/histopathology) to evaluate suspected fungal sinusitis; start or re-start systemic mold-active antifungal therapy per local protocol pending results (e.g., posaconazole or voriconazole/isavuconazole) given high-risk post-transplant status
Plan bone marrow aspirate/biopsy with morphology, flow cytometry and MRD testing (and chimerism) to distinguish relapse vs post-therapy cytopenias

Reinforce and optimize infection prophylaxis/adherence (continue acyclovir; ensure regular TMP-SMX or alternative PJP prophylaxis; review need for mold prophylaxis), reconcile all medications and avoid unsupervised discontinuation
Monitor cytopenias with repeat CBC at short interval and transfuse/support as needed (e.g., platelets/RBCs per thresholds; consider G-CSF depending on ANC trend and treating team practice)
Discuss outpatient relapse-prevention/relapse-treatment pathways once MRD status known (e.g., blinatumomab, inotuzumab, TKIs if Ph+), and assess clinical trial eligibility

content:
Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow biopsy (2024-07-09): The marrow was hypocellular (cellularity 25%) with therapy-related toxic changes. Trilineage hematopoiesis was present but displayed dysplastic features: megakaryopoiesis was reduced with small, hypolobulated forms; erythropoiesis showed megaloblastoid maturation; and granulopoiesis exhibited toxic granulation and nuclear irregularities. There was no blast excess (<5% by morphology).
Flow cytometry revealed a normal distribution of myeloid and lymphoid populations with T-cells showing mild inversion of the CD4/CD8 ratio. A small blast population (0.1%) was detected, characterized by CD19-/CD22-, CD34^dim, CD33-, CD10-, CD20-, CD117+, CD5+, CD7+, CD56-, cyMPO-, cyTdT-, cyCD3-.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Extended diagnostics (ENT/endoscopy findings and sinus cultures/biopsy; fungal markers; CMV/EBV PCR; IgG/lymphocyte subsets; and current antifungal/anti-infective management)"}}

response.request: Extended diagnostics (ENT/endoscopy findings and sinus cultures/biopsy; fungal markers; CMV/EBV PCR; IgG/lymphocyte subsets; and current antifungal/anti-infective management)

content:
Extended Diagnostics:
ENT consultation (2024-07-13) with endoscopic sinus surgery revealed a fungal mass, which was excised completely. Culture grew Aspergillus fumigatus (azole-sensitive). Histology showed typical hyphae. Following infectious disease consultation, antifungal therapy was switched to isavuconazole for outpatient use, and liposomal amphotericin B was reserved for inpatient administration.
Pulmonary function testing showed a mildly reduced DLCO (65% predicted).
Blood cultures were negative.
Viral PCR monitoring: CMV, EBV, and adenovirus all negative.
Serology: HBV, HCV, and HIV negative.
Immunologic profile: CD4⁺ 110/µL, CD8⁺ 320/µL (ratio 0.34); IgG 220 mg/dL.
LP was performed: cerebrospinal fluid was clear and colorless. Cell count 2/µL; protein 42 mg/dL; glucose 68 mg/dL. Cytospin and morphology showed no blasts.
FDG-PET/CT (2024-07-11) demonstrated no hypermetabolic lymphadenopathy or extranodal disease. There was intense FDG uptake in both maxillary sinuses. Previously noted abdominal subcutaneous nodules had resolved. No abnormal uptake was observed in the liver, spleen, bone marrow, or CNS. Mild symmetrical uptake in paraspinal and thigh muscles was attributed to recent physical activity.

low threshold for inpatient liposomal amphotericin B if clinical worsening", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Treat immune deficiency: initiate IVIG replacement for IgG 220 mg/dL (and repeat IgG monitoring), and optimize anti-infective prophylaxis adherence (regular PJP prophylaxis; continue HSV/VZV prophylaxis)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Clarify leukemia status with sensitive MRD testing (NGS/ClonoSEQ or PCR if trackable, plus repeat marrow/flow as needed) and review lineage of the 0.1% atypical blasts; correlate with chimerism and prior immunophenotype when available", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Manage cytopenias/poor marrow function supportively: serial CBCs, transfusion support as needed; consider G-CSF for clinically significant neutropenia/infections; review and adjust myelosuppressive meds (e.g., TMP-SMX alternative if contributing)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "If persistent dysplasia/cytopenias beyond recovery window or worsening counts: pursue myeloid NGS panel and cytogenetics to assess for t-MDS/t-AML and consider referral for transplant/clinical trial discussion as appropriate", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Post–alloSCT and post–CD19 CAR-T patient with hypocellular marrow and persistent cytopenias, no morphologic/flow evidence of B‑ALL relapse, with proven invasive maxillary sinus aspergillosis and significant immune deficiency (CD4 lymphopenia, hypogammaglobulinemia).
differentials: Early/low-level B‑ALL relapse (MRD-positive despite no morphologic relapse)
Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm (t‑MDS) vs transient therapy-related dysplasia in a hypocellular marrow

treatment_plan.action: Continue outpatient mold-active antifungal therapy with isavuconazole (monitor levels if used locally, QT interval, LFTs, drug–drug interactions) and ensure close ENT/ID follow-up after surgical debridement; low threshold for inpatient liposomal amphotericin B if clinical worsening

Treat immune deficiency: initiate IVIG replacement for IgG 220 mg/dL (and repeat IgG monitoring), and optimize anti-infective prophylaxis adherence (regular PJP prophylaxis; continue HSV/VZV prophylaxis)

Clarify leukemia status with sensitive MRD testing (NGS/ClonoSEQ or PCR if trackable, plus repeat marrow/flow as needed) and review lineage of the 0.1% atypical blasts; correlate with chimerism and prior immunophenotype when available

Manage cytopenias/poor marrow function supportively: serial CBCs, transfusion support as needed; consider G-CSF for clinically significant neutropenia/infections; review and adjust myelosuppressive meds (e.g., TMP-SMX alternative if contributing)

If persistent dysplasia/cytopenias beyond recovery window or worsening counts: pursue myeloid NGS panel and cytogenetics to assess for t-MDS/t-AML and consider referral for transplant/clinical trial discussion as appropriate

Setting: Relapse and transplant planning conference in a heavily pretreated leukemia patient
Capabilities: You now have results from bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, clonality assays, and broad molecular profiling together with a comprehensive treatment history including alloSCT, bispecifics, inotuzumab, blinatumomab, and CD19 CAR-T. You are expected to interpret the pattern of MRD relapse, phenotypic evolution, and donor-derived CHIP, and to formulate a realistic outpatient-oriented salvage and transplant-bridging strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content:
Treatment History:
Diagnosis (2020-09-15): Common B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B-ALL), Philadelphia chromosome–negative, CD20 expression 74%. Bone marrow showed 96% blasts, peripheral blood 75% blasts. No CNS involvement. Leukocytes: 21 × 10⁹/L at diagnosis.
Induction therapy (2020-09-22): Rituximab 375 mg/m² (day 1), intrathecal methotrexate 15 mg, vincristine 2 mg absolute, daunorubicin 45 mg/m², PEG-asparaginase 2000 U/m², and dexamethasone 10 mg/m².
Bone marrow on day 22 showed persistent blasts.
Induction II (2020-10-20): Rituximab 375 mg/m²; intrathecal methotrexate 15 mg; cyclophosphamide 1000 mg/m²; cytarabine 75 mg/m²; PEG-asparaginase 1000 U/m² (reduced for hepatic steatosis); and 6-mercaptopurine 60 mg/m² (interrupted for hyperbilirubinemia).
Consolidation I (2020-11-22): Rituximab 375 mg/m² day 60; dexamethasone 10 mg/m² days 61–65; vindesine 3 mg/m² (max. 5 mg) day 61; methotrexate 1500 mg/m² day 1; etoposide phosphate 250 mg/m² days 4–5; cytarabine 2000 mg/m² ×2 day 5; intrathecal triple prophylaxis (Ara-C/MTX/dexa) day 12.
Consolidation II (2020-12-20): Rituximab 375 mg/m² day 0; methotrexate 1500 mg/m² days 1 and 15; 6-mercaptopurine 25 mg/m² days 1–7 and 15–21; PEG-asparaginase 1000 U/m² days 2 and 16; triple intrathecal prophylaxis days 1 and 15.
Re-induction: Prednisolone 3 × 20 mg/m² days 1–14; vindesine 3 mg/m² days 1 and 7; doxorubicin 25 mg/m² days 1 and 7; PEG-asparaginase 1000 U/m² day 14.
Relapse (2021-02-11): Switched to blinatumomab, achieving molecular CR after cycle 1. Subsequent cycles: 2021-03-09 and 2021-03-23.
Allogeneic SCT (2021-04-22): HLA-identical unrelated donor; conditioning with TBI (12 Gy), cyclophosphamide, and ATG. Complications: symptomatic SARS-CoV-2 infection (Delta), sepsis, severe mucositis, transplant-associated TMA, and PRES. Cyclosporine A discontinued. MRI showed cortical and subcortical FLAIR hyperintensities consistent with PRES. Treated with prednisolone 2 mg/kg, gradual taper, and antihypertensives. 2021-05-22: Achieved molecular CR with full donor chimerism.
Molecular relapse (2022-02-15): MRD positivity (0.4%) and 4.8% recipient chimerism. Blinatumomab restarted (2022-03-12) at 28 µg/d, dose-reduced to 9 µg/d for CTCAE grade III fever (40.5 °C), later re-started. EBV reactivation treated with rituximab 375 mg/m² on 2022-04-05 and 2022-04-14. Donor lymphocyte infusions with 1 × 10⁶ CD3+ cells/kg on 06/02/22, DLI 2 with 5 × 10⁶ on 07/03/22, and DLI 3 with 1 × 10⁷ on 08/05/22. This was followed by severe gastrointestinal GVHD, histologically grade III, overall grade IV. GvHD was treated with prednisolone 2 mg/kg, ruxolitinib 10 mg BID, ECP (from 2022-10-02), and etanercept (from 2022-10-12): achieved CR, maintained on low-dose steroids and ruxolitinib.
Relapse occurred on 2022-12-28: The patient developed new palpable abdominal subcutaneous nodules. MRD positivity with 2.1% recipient chimerism. Skin biopsy confirmed leukemic infiltration (CD19⁺, CD22⁺).
Bridging therapy: Inotuzumab ozogamicin: cycle 1 (2023-01-27, 0.8 mg/m²), 2023-02-04 (0.5 mg/m²), 2023-02-11 (0.5 mg/m²).
CAR-T therapy (2023-03-07): Tisagenlecleucel infusion after fludarabine 30 mg/m²/day and cyclophosphamide 500 mg/m²/day (days –5 to –3). Developed grade 2 CRS (fever, hypotension) treated with tocilizumab ×4. Neutropenic fever (2023-03-13): Staphylococcus hominis (CVC) and Staphylococcus epidermidis (port); port explanted. Treated with piperacillin/tazobactam followed by meropenem and vancomycin. The patient achieved molecular and metabolic CR (FDG-PET negative).
2023-09-01: Prolonged cytopenia: CD34⁺ stem cell boost from alloSCT donor (2.43 × 10⁶ CD34⁺/kg).
2023-12-15: Bone marrow MRD 0.01%.
2024-01-15: Bone marrow MRD 0.02%.
2024-02-08: Inotuzumab ozogamicin re-initiated → molecular CR with full chimerism after cycle 2.

repeat marrow at short interval if MRD rising", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Outpatient-oriented salvage/maintenance strategy if MRD becomes positive or rises: prefer CD22-targeted therapy (inotuzumab in fractionated outpatient dosing) given prior responses and post\u2013CD19 CAR-T setting; avoid DLI given prior life-threatening GI GVHD unless compelling indication", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Multiply relapsed Ph-negative common B-ALL after alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T, currently in very deep remission by morphology/standard flow (hypocellular marrow with therapy-related dysplasia/cytopenias) with history of fluctuating low-level MRD post–CAR-T and repeated antigen-positive extramedullary relapse; concurrent profound secondary immune deficiency complicated by proven invasive maxillary sinus aspergillosis.
differentials: Impending MRD relapse of B-ALL below morphologic/standard-flow detection (requires high-sensitivity MRD assay and trend)
Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm (t-MDS) versus transient therapy-related dysplasia in a hypocellular marrow
Donor-derived clonal hematopoiesis contributing to dysplasia/cytopenias

treatment_plan.action: Continue outpatient isavuconazole after surgical debridement for invasive Aspergillus sinusitis; coordinate ENT/ID follow-up and monitor for drug interactions/toxicity (LFTs, QT, interactions with statins/CCBs)

Immune reconstitution/support: start IVIG replacement for hypogammaglobulinemia (IgG 220 mg/dL) and enforce consistent anti-infective prophylaxis (PJP prophylaxis; continue acyclovir); vaccinate per post-transplant schedule when eligible

Define relapse risk precisely: obtain high-sensitivity marrow MRD (NGS/PCR using prior clone), correlate with chimerism trend, and compare immunophenotype with prior relapses (CD19/CD22) to detect antigen escape; repeat marrow at short interval if MRD rising

Outpatient-oriented salvage/maintenance strategy if MRD becomes positive or rises: prefer CD22-targeted therapy (inotuzumab in fractionated outpatient dosing) given prior responses and post–CD19 CAR-T setting; consider blinatumomab only if CD19 expression returns and neurotoxicity risk acceptable; refer for clinical trial (e.g., CD22 CAR-T, CD19/22 dual targeting, menin/other targeted agents if mutations)

Transplant/cellular-therapy planning: if MRD relapse confirmed, pursue bridge to a consolidative cellular strategy (second alloSCT vs second CAR-T/dual-target trial) based on donor options, GVHD history, organ function, and infection control; avoid DLI given prior life-threatening GI GVHD unless compelling indication
